penalty_extra_disagreement.analysis: Eighth video where Claude applies out-of-rubric penalty bookkeeping (V20, V22, V26, V28, V29, V30, V31, postV10). Uniquely, postV10 also triggers Claude's gap penalty because Claude reads the final frame as showing a partial slit gap while GPT-4o reads it as complete closure. This makes postV10 the largest penalty-delta case in the series at 10.0 points.

phase_attribution_disagreement.claude_diagnosis: Phase split: idle 5s, load 13s, placement 67s, first 23s, second 22s, third 35s, cut 50s. Placement and cutting are the dominant loss centers; both are the worst in the post-training series.
phase_attribution_disagreement.gpt4o_diagnosis: Phase split: load 16.5s, placement 38s, first 30s, second 40s, third 45s, cut 36.2s, completion 6s. Throws 2 and 3 are the dominant loss centers; placement and cut are moderate.
phase_attribution_disagreement.analysis: Same INVERSE cut-phase boundary pattern as V30/V31 but with an additional placement-boundary disagreement. Claude's 67s placement vs GPT-4o's 38s is the largest placement-phase gap in the series (29s). Claude's 50s cut vs GPT-4o's 36.2s cut adds another ~14s to the cut side. Tails converge within 3.3s but the internal allocation is dramatically different. Same convergent-tails pattern seen across V28/V30/V31 — the cut and placement phase boundaries are the two weakest points in the current prompt definition.

drain_gap_divergence_analysis.claude_finding: Visible gap in upper portion of slit; knot compact but closure only partial.
drain_gap_divergence_analysis.gpt4o_finding: No visible gap; slit approximation complete; knot seated and stable.
drain_gap_divergence_analysis.significance: This is the first drain-gap divergence recorded outside the V11-V20 early-training window and the first divergence on a post-training video. The running tally is now AGREE 11, DIVERGE 6 (V11, V14, V16, V17, V20, postV10). Unlike the earlier divergence cases which were on monitor-recorded videos with artifact noise, postV10 is on the standard training station. Worth flagging for human review if a third opinion is available — the gap/no-gap call has a direct effect on 8 points of penalty bookkeeping in Claude's scoring.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: Moderate score agreement with delta 13.3 FLS. Reconciled value is 385.8 (GPT-4o, rubric-strict). The 13.3-point delta breaks down as: 3.3s time advantage for GPT-4o (contributes -3.3) + 10.0 penalty advantage for GPT-4o (contributes +10.0) = net +13.3. If Claude's gap finding is correct, the reconciled score should be lower; if GPT-4o's no-gap finding is correct, 385.8 stands. Pending drain-gap adjudication, 385.8 is the rubric-strict value.

systematic_findings.largest_penalty_delta_in_series: 10.0 penalty delta is the largest recorded. Driven by both Claude's persistent out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty AND Claude's gap-based deduction. postV10 is the only post-training video that has triggered both forms of Claude-side penalty simultaneously.
systematic_findings.rubric_scope_issue_now_8_of_17: Eighth out-of-rubric case across 17 two-teacher-scored videos. Pattern is definitively a prompt drift on Claude's side. v002 prompt should formalize the rubric-allowed penalty set explicitly and move quality/efficiency observations into a separate cosmetic_quality field.
systematic_findings.drain_gap_divergence_reappears: postV10 is the first drain-gap divergence outside the early-training V11-V20 window. Running tally: AGREE 11, DIVERGE 6. Unlike earlier divergences which were video-quality driven, postV10 is on the standard station — the disagreement is about visual interpretation of the final frame, not recording artifacts.
systematic_findings.inverse_phase_pattern_persists: Third video (V30, V31, postV10) where the convergent-tails pattern shows Claude bundling into cut and GPT-4o spreading into throws. Also the first case adding a placement-boundary disagreement (67s vs 38s). Cut-phase and placement-phase start definitions are now confirmed as the two highest-priority prompt fixes.
systematic_findings.postV10_as_series_low: Both teachers independently rank postV10 in the bottom tier of post-training runs. Claude calls it the worst in the series (372.5) framing it as late-session fatigue or skill regression. GPT-4o scores it 385.8 framing it as competent-but-inefficient. The split reflects the gap-finding disagreement: with the gap, this is a problem run; without it, it's a slow but clean run.
